semanticCore: Defeating, routing, chasing away, valley, hollow, or press
quranicRelevance: Routing armies of disbelief (Mahzoom - 'sayuhzamu al-jam'u', 'jundun... mahzoomun').

keyDerivations.meaning: To defeat, route, conquer, crush, or disperse a force

figurativeOrRareUsages: جُنْدٌ مَا هُنَالِكَ مَهْزُومٌ مِنَ الْأَحْزَابِ (38:11): (They are but) a defeated army of the factions there (prophecy of Mecca's military defeat).
discardedNoise: Pressing hollows in soft leather shoes.
The historical well of Zamzam (Hazmat Jibreel) in post-canonical logs.
summaryEn: The root represents military defeat (Hazm), predicting the eventual rout (Mahzoom) of pagan coalitions and celebrating historical victories over oppressors.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الكسر والفرار والدحر العسكري (هزم)، متنبئاً بكسر حشود قريش والأحزاب أمام قوة الإيمان (سيهزم الجمع - جند ما هنالك مهزوم).
rawText: اِنْهَزَمَ 7 It (an army) was routed, discomfited, defeated, or put to flight. (K, &c.) See حَاصَ, in art. حيص; and اِنْحَازَ, in art. حوز; from both of which it is distinguished. مَهْزُومُ الصَّدْرِ Depressed of breast, i. e., apparently, illiberal, niggardly: see حَوْضٌ. هَزْمَةٌ The [purring, or] sound of the throat of a cat. (TA.) === The pit between the two collar-bones. (TA, art. ترب.) === [The pit above a horse's eye.] (K, voce وَقْبٌ.) See also خُنْعُبَةٌ, and قَلْتَةٌ: and see عُقْمٌ, where it seems to mean a stricture: it generally and properly signifies a depression, or dint: or a pit, or small hollow, resembling a dint: see also غَيْبٌ.
